boundary_flags: mixed_start: page A_0190_R begins with a prior trip entry ('Sunday 25th August' – Peter to Beauchamp music camp); the 1996 trip starts at 'Friday Sept 13th'.
mixed_end: the trip diary continues beyond A_0190_R; page A_0191_L is a photo caption from an unrelated Ireland trip. Content about Malvern and the trip's end is not captured in the available pages.
Date mismatch: the transcribed ISO dates on A_0190_R show 1991 (transcriber default year), but 'Friday Sept 13th' fell on a Friday in 1996 and the trip_id is 1996; correct year is 1996.
summary: A September 1996 trip from Birmingham: the family set off early on Friday the 13th, made a brief transit stop at WoodEnd to visit friends, then drove to a campsite at Stamford Bridge (East Yorkshire, near York). The title also references Malvern and William's birthday but the diary text for those legs is not preserved in the available pages.
extraction_notes: Page A_0190_R is boundary-mixed. The 'Sunday 25th August' section belongs to a preceding trip about Peter joining the Portals at Beauchamp music camp; it has been excluded. The relevant 1996 trip content begins at 'Friday Sept 13th'. The ISO dates on the page were wrongly assigned to 1991 by the transcriber; the correct year is 1996 (Sept 13 fell on a Friday that year). Page A_0191_L belongs to an entirely different Ireland trip (Great Blasket Island / Dingle) and contributes no evidence to this route. Malvern appears only in the title_guess, not in surviving diary text, and has been recorded as an inferred_anchor with low confidence. Thomas is not mentioned in the departure text; the travellers list is drawn from the title context and family convention. The '[?***]' after 'Stamford Bridge' suggests illegible text, probably the campsite/farm name.
